title: row height doesn't apply to row
author: dong-qian
body:
Default row height does not apply to the excel generated.
```
 let infoSheet = this.workbook.addWorksheet("Info", {properties:{ defaultRowHeight: 50 }});
```
or
```
infoSheet.properties.defaultRowHeight = 50;
```
![rowheight](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/24208956/31632960-d0931590-b28c-11e7-85fe-ceccdfe39bb8.jpg)

comments.author: theangeloumali
comments.body:
I use this and it works.
sheet.properties.defaultRowHeight = 40;

comments.author: JoeyAspire
chenvan
comments.body: the same problem, just want to add a min height style.

comments.author: ScottPeterJohnson
comments.body:
For other people having this issue: Are you first opening the generated excel files on the Windows version of Excel 2016?
I've seen something on that platform where Excel will do weird resizing of rows on a worksheet. I painstakingly tracked this down to exceljs not generating a <sheetView/> element, causing Excel to flip. I fixed this by adding an empty view to all of my worksheets via `worksheet.views = [{}]`

comments.author: yuanoook
comments.body:
@ScottPeterJohnson  thanks very much.

`worksheet.views = [{}]` works for me

comments.author: byeokim
comments.body:
I experienced the same "weird resizing of rows". (FWIW I had first opened the exceljs-generated `.xlsx` file on the macOS version of MS Excel) And I found that MS Excel-generated `.xlsx` file has the following XML element in `xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml` and @ScottPeterJohnson's `worksheet.views = [{}]` also adds the element:

```xml
...

<!-- <dimension ... /> -->
<sheetViews>
  <sheetView workbookViewId="0" />
</sheetViews>
<!-- <sheetFormatPr ... /> -->

...
```

This addition seems to solve the issue but I couldn't find any explanation of that from [MS Documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/documentformat.openxml.spreadsheet.sheetview?view=openxml-2.8.1):

> **workbookViewId (Workbook View Index):** Zero-based index of this workbook view, pointing to a workbookView element in the bookViews collection. The possible values for this attribute are defined by the W3C XML Schema unsignedInt datatype.

So for now I can't propose to make it a default option for `worksheet.views` which can be done by editing below code:

https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/c66504132d51fd2fcc31a2e69735532fa4a5829f/lib/doc/worksheet.js#L110
